```crystal
require "string"

# Helper function to check if a character is a lowercase English letter
fn is_letter?(c : Char) : Bool
  c >= 'a' && c <= 'z'
end

# Helper function to check if a character is a delimiter (anything not a letter or hyphen)
fn is_delimiter?(c : Char) : Bool
  !(is_letter?(c) || c == '-')
end

def solve(chunks : Array(String), queries : Array(String)) : Array(Int64)
  # 1. Concatenate chunks
  s = chunks.join("")
  n = s.length

  # 2. Preprocess s into s_prime by replacing invalid characters/hyphens with spaces
  s_prime_chars = Array(Char).new(n)

  for i in 0...n
    c = s[i]

    if is_letter?(c)
      s_prime_chars[i] = c
    elsif is_delimiter?(c)
      # Any character that is not a letter or hyphen acts as a separator
      s_prime_chars[i] = ' '
    elsif c == '-'
      # Check if the hyphen is surrounded by letters in the original string s
      is_preceded_by_letter = i > 0 && is_letter?(s[i - 1])
      is_followed_by_letter = i < n - 1 && is_letter?(s[i + 1])

      if is_preceded_by_letter && is_followed_by_letter
        s_prime_chars[i] = '-'
      else
        # Invalid hyphen acts as a separator
        s_prime_chars[i] = ' '
      end
    end
  end

  s_prime = s_prime_chars.join("")

  # 3. Tokenize s_prime into a list of valid words
  words = []
  current_word = String.new
  
  for char in s_prime
    if char >= 'a' && char <= 'z' || char == '-'
      # Assuming hyphens here are only valid if they passed the preprocessing step
      # and are now adjacent to letters.
      current_word << char
    else
      # Delimiter found (space)
      if current_word.length > 0
        words << current_word.dup
      end
      current_word = String.new
    end
  end
  # Handle potential word at the end of the string
  if current_word.length > 0
    words << current_word
  end

  # 4. Count frequencies
  word_counts = Hash(String, Int64).new
  for word in words
    word_counts[word] ||= 0
    word_counts[word] += 1
  end

  # 5. Construct the result array ans
  ans = Array(Int64).new(queries.length)
  for i in 0...queries.length
    query = queries[i]
    ans[i] = word_counts[query] || 0
  end

  ans
end
```

  for word in words

require "string"

class Solver
    def solve(chunks : Array(String), queries : Array(String)) : Array(Int64)
        s = chunks.join

        # Pre-calculate the count of every unique word found in s
        word_counts = Hash[String, Int64].new

        s_len = s.length

        # Helper function to check if a character is a lowercase letter
        def is_letter(char : Char) : Bool
            char >= 'a' && char <= 'z'
        end

        # Helper function to check if a hyphen at index i is validly surrounded by letters in s
        def is_valid_hyphen(s : String, i : Int) : Bool
            s_len = s.length
            
            # Check left neighbor
            left_is_letter = i > 0 && is_letter(s[i - 1])
            
            # Check right neighbor
            right_is_letter = i < s_len - 1 && is_letter(s[i + 1])
            
            left_is_letter && right_is_letter
        end

        current_word = String.new

        for i in 0...s_len
            char = s[i]

            if is_letter(char)
                # It's always part of a potential word
                current_word.push(char)
            elsif char == '-'
                if is_valid_hyphen(s, i)
                    # Valid hyphen, part of the word
                    current_word.push(char)
                else
                    # Invalid hyphen acts as a separator
                    if current_word.length > 0
                        word_counts[current_word] = (word_counts[current_word] || 0) + 1
                        current_word = String.new
                    end
                end
            else
                # Space or other delimiter character
                if current_word.length > 0
                    word_counts[current_word] = (word_counts[current_word] || 0) + 1
                    current_word = String.new
                end
            end
        end

        # Process any word remaining at the end of the string
        if current_word.length > 0
            word_counts[current_word] = (word_counts[current_word] || 0) + 1
        end

        # Generate the result array
        ans = Array(Int64).new(queries.length)
        for j in 0...queries.length
            query = queries[j]
            ans[j] = word_counts[query] || 0
        end

        ans
    end
end

            current_word_chars << char_j

          else

        else
          # Any other character (space, punctuation, etc.) acts as separator
          break
      end
      
      # Construct the maximal word found
      word = current_word_chars.join("")
      all_words << word
      
      # Move i to the character immediately following the extracted word
      i = j
    else
      # s[i] is a delimiter, skip it
      i += 1
    end
  end
  
  # Count occurrences for each query
  query_counts = Hash(String, Int64).new
  queries.each do |q|
    query_counts[q] = 0
  end
  
  all_words.each do |word|
    queries.each do |q|
      if q == word
        query_counts[q] += 1
      end
    end
  end
  
  # Return results in the order of queries
  result = Array(Int64).new(queries.size)
  queries.each_with_index do |q, index|
    result[index] = query_counts[q]
  end
  
  result
end

# Helper function to check if a character at index i in string s is a word character
fn is_word_char(s : String, i : Int) : Bool
  len = s.length
  char = s[i]
  
  if char.is_ascii_lowercase?

  elsif char == '-'
    # Check surrounding characters
    has_left = i > 0 && s[i-1].is_ascii_lowercase?
    has_right = i < len - 1 && s[i+1].is_ascii_lowercase?
    return has_left && has_right
  else

def solve(chunks : Array(String), queries : Array(String)) : Array(Int64)

      word = s[start_index...j]

require "string"

# Helper function to check if a character at index i in string s is a word character
fn is_word_char(s : String, i : Int) : Bool
  len = s.length
  char = s[i]
  
  if char.is_ascii_lowercase?
    return true
  elsif char == '-'
    # A hyphen is valid only if it is surrounded by lowercase letters.
    has_left = i > 0 && s[i-1].is_ascii_lowercase?
    has_right = i < len - 1 && s[i+1].is_ascii_lowercase?
    return has_left && has_right
  else
    return false
  end
end

def solve(chunks : Array(String), queries : Array(String)) : Array(Int64)
  # 1. Concatenate chunks
  s = chunks.join("")
  
  word_counts = Hash(String, Int64)
  len = s.length
  i = 0
  
  # 2. Extract all valid maximal words (tokens)
  while i < len
    # Check if a word starts here
    if is_word_char(s, i)
      start_index = i
      j = i
      
      # Greedily extend the token as long as the characters remain word characters
      while j < len && is_word_char(s, j)
        j += 1
      end
      
      # Token found: s[start_index...j-1]
      word = s[start_index...j]
      
      # Count the word
      word_counts[word] = (word_counts[word] || 0) + 1
      
      # Move i past the found word
      i = j
    else
      # Not a word character, skip this character (it's a delimiter)
      i += 1
    end
  end
  
  # 3. Generate results for queries
  ans = Array(Int64, queries.size)
  queries.each_with_index do |q, k|
    # Look up the count, defaulting to 0 if the word was never found
    ans[k] = word_counts[q] || 0
  end
  
  return ans
end
